steps.title: Company Overview
steps.content:
NCR Atleos generates $3.7B in annual revenue from ATM and self-service banking solutions. The company operates globally with 20,000 employees across 160 countries, managing 650,000 ATMs and processing 13 billion transactions annually. Following its 2023 spin-off from NCR Corporation, Atleos maintains the world's largest ATM network and provides managed services to financial institutions.

The company serves 1,300 financial institution clients across retail banking (67% of revenue), enterprise ATM outsourcing (23%), and network services (10%) segments. Average contract duration spans 5.2 years with 91% client retention. Transaction processing operates on a per-transaction fee model with average margins of 18% on managed services contracts.

Atleos faces a $127M annual burden from manual ATM maintenance coordination. Field service teams spend 142,000 hours per year coordinating technician dispatch, parts inventory, and service completion across fragmented regional systems. This workload creates 8-day average delays on non-critical service requests and prevents proactive maintenance optimization that could reduce ATM downtime by 23%.

The company confronts $89M in annual costs from compliance documentation across 47 banking jurisdictions. Legal and compliance teams spend 94,000 hours annually updating regulatory filings, audit documentation, and cross-border transaction monitoring reports. This burden diverts senior compliance officers from strategic risk assessment and creates 31-day backlogs on routine regulatory inquiries.

Cash forecasting inefficiencies cost Atleos $43M annually in excess working capital. Treasury analysts spend 67,000 hours per year reconciling ATM cash levels, deposit patterns, and replenishment schedules across decentralized systems. Poor cash positioning creates $890M in excess cash inventory earning minimal returns while geographic cash shortages require expensive emergency replenishments.

Financial figures derive from 2024 10-K filings, Q4 2025 earnings releases, and investor presentations. Operational burden estimates apply $150/hour fully-loaded rates for professional staff, $100/hour for technical specialists, and $60/hour for field service coordinators. Data maturity assessed at Level 2 based on disclosed ERP modernization initiatives and the company's ongoing post-spin integration of fragmented legacy systems.

data.Target State: Automated dispatch with predictive maintenance, 3-day response time, 12% downtime reduction through proactive intervention
data.Current State: 142,000 annual hours on manual dispatch coordination, 8-day average response time for non-critical repairs, 23% preventable downtime from reactive maintenance

data.Target State: Automated compliance reporting with continuous monitoring, 7-day inquiry response, real-time audit trail generation
data.Current State: 94,000 annual hours on compliance documentation, 31-day backlog on regulatory inquiries, manual audit trail assembly across 47 jurisdictions

data.Target State: Predictive cash positioning, $290M inventory reduction, automated replenishment optimization
data.Current State: $890M excess cash inventory, 67,000 hours on manual forecasting, geographic cash imbalances requiring emergency replenishments

data.Target State: AI-assisted resolution for 72% of routine queries, 4-hour response time, escalation rate below 12%
data.Current State: 78,000 annual hours on routine inquiry handling, 18-hour average response time, 34% of queries require escalation due to knowledge gaps

data.Target State: Real-time transaction pattern analysis, 6-hour fraud detection, 4% false positive rate
data.Current State: 56,000 hours on manual transaction monitoring, 72-hour fraud detection cycles, 14% false positive rate on anomaly alerts

steps.title: Strategic Anchoring & Business Drivers
steps.content: Five strategic themes anchor NCR Atleos's AI opportunity across operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and service delivery transformation. These themes span technology operations, financial management, compliance, customer service, and supply chain domains. Each theme connects to specific P&L impact through cost reduction, working capital optimization, and revenue protection from service quality improvements.

steps.title: Business Function Inventory & KPI Baselines
steps.content: Twelve critical business functions establish baseline performance metrics across ATM operations, financial management, compliance, and customer service domains. Each KPI links to strategic themes and provides industry benchmark context across average, industry-best, and overall-best performance tiers.

data.Friction Point: Manual ATM service dispatch across 650,000 units with fragmented regional systems requiring coordinator synthesis of technician availability, parts inventory, and service priority
Regulatory compliance documentation across 47 banking jurisdictions requiring manual assembly of transaction logs, audit trails, and cross-border reporting
Cash forecasting across 160 countries with manual reconciliation of ATM withdrawal patterns, deposit schedules, and seasonal demand variations
Customer technical inquiry resolution requiring manual knowledge base search across product documentation, service bulletins, and historical ticket databases
Transaction anomaly monitoring across 13B annual transactions with manual pattern analysis and fraud investigation coordination
Service parts inventory optimization requiring manual demand forecasting across 340 SKUs and 67 regional distribution centers
Contract renewal risk assessment requiring manual analysis of service performance data, customer satisfaction metrics, and competitive intelligence
Software deployment validation across diverse ATM hardware configurations requiring manual test case execution and regression analysis
Financial close process requiring manual consolidation of regional P&L data, intercompany eliminations, and variance analysis across 47 reporting entities
Employee onboarding documentation requiring manual assembly of role-specific training materials, compliance certifications, and system access procedures
Vendor performance evaluation requiring manual scoring of delivery timeliness, quality metrics, and commercial terms across 420 suppliers
Marketing campaign performance analysis requiring manual consolidation of engagement metrics, conversion funnels, and attribution modeling across digital channels

steps.title: Friction Point Mapping
steps.content: Twelve operational bottlenecks quantify annual cost burdens across field service coordination, compliance documentation, treasury operations, customer support, and network monitoring. Each friction point maps to specific business functions and strategic themes with severity ratings reflecting business impact.

data.Description: Automated service request triage and technician dispatch using real-time analysis of technician skill sets, geographic proximity, parts inventory at regional depots, and ATM criticality scoring. System evaluates 340 SKUs across 67 distribution centers to recommend optimal dispatch assignments with parts pre-allocation.
data.AI Primitives: Workflow Automation, Data Analysis, Research & Information Retrieval
data.Target Friction: Manual ATM service dispatch across 650,000 units with fragmented regional systems requiring coordinator synthesis of technician availability, parts inventory, and service priority
Desired Outcomes: Reduce mean time to repair from 8 days to 3 days for non-critical service requests
Increase first-time fix rate from 74% to 91% through improved parts pre-allocation
Deflect 78,000 annual coordinator hours from manual dispatch to strategic capacity planning
Reduce emergency parts shipments by 34% through predictive inventory positioning
data.Pattern Rationale: Central orchestrator evaluates service requests and delegates to specialist agents for technician matching, parts availability checking, and route optimization. Each specialist maintains domain expertise while the orchestrator ensures optimal dispatch decisions across competing constraints.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Service coordinator validates AI dispatch recommendations for critical ATM outages affecting high-transaction locations before technician assignment

data.Description: Automated generation of jurisdiction-specific regulatory filings and audit documentation by synthesizing transaction logs, compliance policies, and historical filing templates. System monitors 47 banking jurisdictions to produce draft reports with citation links to source data and regulatory framework references.
data.AI Primitives: Content Creation, Research & Information Retrieval, Data Analysis
data.Target Friction: Regulatory compliance documentation across 47 banking jurisdictions requiring manual assembly of transaction logs, audit trails, and cross-border reporting
Desired Outcomes: Reduce regulatory filing cycle time from 31 days to 7 days through automated draft generation
Deflect 52,000 annual compliance hours from manual documentation to strategic risk assessment
Decrease audit findings per review from 8.4 to 3.2 through consistent documentation standards
Eliminate 100% of manual citation assembly across transaction evidence
data.Pattern Rationale: Generator agent drafts compliance reports from transaction data while critic agent validates against regulatory requirements and precedent filings. The two-agent loop ensures documentation meets jurisdiction-specific standards before human compliance officer review, reducing manual drafting cycles.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Compliance officer reviews AI-generated regulatory filings for accuracy and completeness before submission to regulatory authorities

data.Description: Machine learning forecasts of ATM cash demand by location, analyzing historical withdrawal patterns, seasonal variations, local events, and economic indicators. System recommends optimal cash replenishment schedules to minimize excess inventory while preventing stockouts across 650,000 ATM locations.
data.Target Friction: Cash forecasting across 160 countries with manual reconciliation of ATM withdrawal patterns, deposit schedules, and seasonal demand variations
Desired Outcomes: Increase cash forecasting accuracy from 73% to 91% through pattern recognition
Reduce cash inventory from $890M to $600M through optimized positioning
Deflect 37,000 treasury analyst hours from manual forecasting to strategic liquidity management
Decrease emergency replenishments by 47% through predictive demand modeling
data.Pattern Rationale: Agent generates initial cash forecasts then critiques predictions against actual withdrawal patterns and seasonal trends. Self-refinement loop iteratively improves forecast accuracy by identifying systematic biases and adjusting for local market dynamics before treasury approval.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Treasury manager validates AI cash positioning recommendations for high-value locations before authorizing armored car replenishment schedules

data.Description: AI-assisted resolution of customer technical inquiries through semantic search across product manuals, service bulletins, troubleshooting guides, and 8 years of historical support tickets. System generates draft responses with source citations and escalation recommendations based on inquiry complexity and customer contract tier.
data.AI Primitives: Research & Information Retrieval, Conversational Interfaces, Content Creation
data.Target Friction: Customer technical inquiry resolution requiring manual knowledge base search across product documentation, service bulletins, and historical ticket databases
Desired Outcomes: Reduce customer inquiry response time from 18 hours to 4 hours through instant knowledge retrieval
Increase first contact resolution rate from 66% to 88% through comprehensive solution synthesis
Deflect 43,000 support specialist hours from knowledge search to complex troubleshooting
Decrease escalation rate from 34% to 12% through improved initial resolution quality
data.Pattern Rationale: Agent queries multiple knowledge sources including product documentation, service bulletins, and historical tickets to synthesize resolution guidance. Tool-use pattern enables semantic search across unstructured documentation and structured case databases to surface relevant solutions for support specialist validation.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Support specialist reviews AI-drafted responses for technical accuracy and customer context before sending resolution to client

data.Description: Continuous monitoring of 13 billion annual ATM transactions to identify unusual patterns indicative of fraud, skimming, or network compromise. System analyzes transaction velocity, geographic distribution, amount patterns, and device behavior to generate risk-scored alerts with supporting evidence for security team investigation.
data.Target Friction: Transaction anomaly monitoring across 13B annual transactions with manual pattern analysis and fraud investigation coordination
Desired Outcomes: Reduce fraud detection cycle time from 72 hours to 6 hours through real-time pattern analysis
Decrease false positive rate from 14% to 4% through adaptive learning models
Deflect 31,000 analyst hours from manual transaction review to strategic threat assessment
Reduce fraud losses by $18M annually through faster detection and response
data.Pattern Rationale: Agent reasons about transaction patterns then acts by flagging anomalies for investigation. ReAct loop allows adaptive exploration of fraud indicators through iterative hypothesis testing, adjusting detection thresholds based on emerging attack vectors before analyst confirmation.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Security analyst validates AI-flagged transaction anomalies above $50K exposure before initiating ATM shutdown or card blocking procedures

data.Description: Machine learning forecasts of service parts demand across 340 SKUs and 67 regional distribution centers, analyzing failure rates by ATM model, technician utilization patterns, and supplier lead times. System recommends stock transfers and purchase orders to maintain target service levels while minimizing inventory carrying costs.
data.Target Friction: Service parts inventory optimization requiring manual demand forecasting across 340 SKUs and 67 regional distribution centers
Desired Outcomes: Increase parts inventory turnover from 3.2x to 5.8x through demand-driven positioning
Reduce stockouts by 52% through predictive replenishment
Deflect 27,000 analyst hours from manual forecasting to supplier quality management
Decrease inventory carrying costs by $8.4M through optimized stock levels
data.Pattern Rationale: Agent decomposes inventory optimization into sub-tasks: demand forecasting by region, lead time analysis by supplier, and stock level calculation by SKU. Sequential planning ensures coordinated inventory positioning across 67 distribution centers before supply chain manager approval.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Supply chain manager validates AI stock transfer recommendations above $100K value before authorizing inter-depot shipments

data.Description: Predictive modeling of contract renewal risk across 1,300 financial institution clients using service performance data, customer satisfaction scores, competitive win/loss intelligence, and contract terms analysis. System generates risk-scored renewal forecasts with recommended retention interventions 180 days before contract expiration.
data.Target Friction: Contract renewal risk assessment requiring manual analysis of service performance data, customer satisfaction metrics, and competitive intelligence
Desired Outcomes: Increase contract renewal rate from 91% to 95% through early risk identification
Deflect 23,000 account manager hours from manual analysis to strategic relationship building
Reduce revenue churn by $34M through proactive retention interventions
Identify 94% of at-risk contracts 6 months before expiration versus 67% currently
data.Pattern Rationale: Specialized agents analyze service performance, customer satisfaction, and competitive intelligence independently then coordinate findings. Multi-agent architecture enables parallel analysis of renewal risk factors with synthesis into unified risk assessment for account manager review.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Account manager reviews AI renewal risk assessments and validates recommended retention strategies before initiating customer engagement

data.Description: Automated generation and execution of software test cases across diverse ATM hardware configurations, analyzing deployment requirements, configuration matrices, and historical defect patterns. System creates test scripts, executes validation runs, and synthesizes results with defect linkage for engineering review.
data.Target Friction: Software deployment validation across diverse ATM hardware configurations requiring manual test case execution and regression analysis
Desired Outcomes: Reduce deployment validation time from 45 days to 12 days through automated test generation
Increase test coverage from 67% to 94% across hardware configuration matrix
Deflect 21,000 developer hours from manual testing to feature development
Decrease post-deployment defects by 58% through comprehensive regression testing
data.Pattern Rationale: Generator creates test cases from deployment requirements while critic validates coverage against ATM configuration matrix. Two-agent quality loop ensures comprehensive regression testing across hardware variants before QA engineer approval and production deployment.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: QA engineer validates AI-generated test coverage and reviews critical path test results before approving production software deployment

data.Description: Automated consolidation of regional P&L data, intercompany eliminations, and variance analysis across 47 reporting entities. System reconciles inter-entity transactions, generates variance commentary with drill-down links to source transactions, and produces executive summary dashboards with KPI trend analysis.
data.AI Primitives: Data Analysis, Content Creation, Workflow Automation
data.Target Friction: Financial close process requiring manual consolidation of regional P&L data, intercompany eliminations, and variance analysis across 47 reporting entities
Desired Outcomes: Reduce financial close cycle from 12 days to 5 days through automated consolidation
Decrease consolidation errors by 73% through systematic elimination logic
Deflect 18,000 analyst hours from manual consolidation to strategic FP&A analysis
Improve variance analysis depth with transaction-level drill-down capability
data.Pattern Rationale: Central orchestrator coordinates specialist agents for regional data consolidation, intercompany elimination calculations, and variance analysis commentary. Orchestrator ensures sequential task completion with data validation gates before controller approval of monthly close package.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: Corporate controller validates AI-generated consolidation entries and reviews variance explanations before finalizing monthly financial statements

data.Description: Automated generation of role-specific onboarding documentation including training schedules, compliance certifications, system access procedures, and equipment checklists. System analyzes job role requirements, department-specific policies, and regulatory training mandates to create personalized 90-day onboarding plans with milestone tracking.
data.AI Primitives: Content Creation, Research & Information Retrieval, Workflow Automation
data.Target Friction: Employee onboarding documentation requiring manual assembly of role-specific training materials, compliance certifications, and system access procedures
Desired Outcomes: Reduce onboarding documentation preparation from 8 hours to 45 minutes per new hire
Increase onboarding plan consistency to 98% compliance with company standards
Deflect 16,000 HR specialist hours from documentation assembly to strategic talent development
Decrease time-to-productivity for new hires by 23% through comprehensive onboarding plans
data.Pattern Rationale: Agent queries role-specific training databases, compliance certification systems, and IT provisioning tools to synthesize personalized onboarding packages. Tool-use pattern enables integration with multiple HR systems to gather requirements and generate customized documentation for HR specialist validation.
data.Human-in-the-Loop Checkpoint: HR specialist reviews AI-generated onboarding documentation for role-specific accuracy and compliance completeness before sending to new hire

steps.title: AI Use Case Generation
steps.content: Ten AI use cases reshape operational processes across field service coordination, compliance automation, treasury optimization, customer support, and network intelligence. Each use case targets specific friction points with human-in-the-loop validation at critical decision points and maps to standardized AI primitives and agentic patterns.

steps.title: Benefits Quantification by Driver
steps.content: Financial benefits quantified across four value drivers using conservative estimation frameworks with explicit formulas. All calculations apply mandatory reduction factors and data maturity adjustments with probability weighting for implementation risk.

steps.title: Readiness & Token Modeling
steps.content: Readiness assessment evaluates organizational capacity, data quality, technical infrastructure, and governance maturity across four dimensions. Token consumption estimates based on Claude 3.5 Sonnet pricing at $3 per 1M input tokens and $15 per 1M output tokens with conservative usage projections.
